Any single gate failure\n1390\t    # blocks PR merge. Without it, license violations, banned dependencies, or\n1391\t    # source registry issues silently slip past code review.\n1392\t    #\n1393\t    # Bead: pdftract-1rljr\n1394\t    # Plan section: Phase 0.4 Quality Targets\n1395\t    #\n1396\t    # Enforcement policy:\n1397\t    # - Licenses: Only MIT, Apache-2.0, BSD-2/3-Clause, ISC, Zlib, Unicode-DFS-2016 allowed\n1398\t    # - GPL/AGPL/LGPL are denied (copyleft contamination)\n1399\t    # - MPL-2.0 exceptions require ADR documentation (cbindgen, option-ext)\n1400\t    # - Bans: Wildcard dependencies denied, duplicate versions warned\n1401\t    # - Advisories: Yanked crates denied, RustSec advisories denied (with exceptions)\n1402\t    # - Sources: Unknown registries and git sources denied\n1403\t    # - deny.toml maintains the policy configuration and exceptions\n1404\t    - name: cargo-deny\n1405\t      activeDeadlineSeconds: 300\n1406\t      container:\n1407\t        image: ronaldraygun/pdftract-test-glibc:1.78\n1408\t        command: [bash, -c]\n1409\t        args:\n1410\t          - |\n1411\t            set -eo pipefail\n1412\t\n1413\t            echo \"==========================================\"\n1414\t            echo \"License, Ban, Source, Advisory Check (cargo-deny)\"\n1415\t            echo \"==========================================\"\n1416\t\n1417\t            cd /workspace\n1418\t            export CARGO_HOME=\"/cache/cargo/registry\"\n1419\t\n1420\t            # Install cargo-deny if not present\n1421\t            if ! command -v cargo-deny &> /dev/null; then\n1422\t              echo \"Installing cargo-deny...\"\n1423\t              cargo install cargo-deny --locked\n1424\t            fi\n1425\t\n1426\t            echo \"=== Running cargo deny check ===\"\n1427\t            echo \"Checks: licenses, bans, sources, advisories\"\n1428\t            echo \"Configuration: deny.toml (policy and exceptions)\"\n1429\t\n1430\t            # Run all checks in one command\n1431\t            # Note: cargo-deny returns exit code 1 for warnings and 2 for errors/denials\n1432\t            # We treat warnings (duplicate versions) as PASS, actual denials as FAIL\n1433\t            OUTPUT=$(cargo deny check \\\n1434\t              licenses bans sources advisories \\\n1435\t              2>&1) || EXIT_CODE=$?\n1436\t\n1437\t            echo \"$OUTPUT\"\n1438\t\n1439\t            # Parse output to determine if there are actual denials (not just warnings)\n1440\t            # Denials contain \"error[\" prefix, warnings contain \"warning[\" prefix\n1441\t            if echo \"$OUTPUT\" | grep -q \"error\\[\"; then\n1442\t              echo \"==========================================\"\n1443\t              echo \"CARGO DENY CHECKS FAILED\"\n1444\t              echo \"==========================================\"\n1445\t\n1446\t              echo \"\"\n1447\t              echo \"One or more checks were denied:\"\n1448\t              echo \"  - licenses: Dependency license violations\"\n1449\t              echo \"  - bans: Banned crates (not duplicate version warnings)\"\n1450\t              echo \"  - sources: Unknown registries or git sources\"\n1451\t              echo \"  - advisories: Security vulnerabilities (RustSec)\"\n1452\t              echo \"\"\n1453\t              echo \"Review the error output above for specific violations.\"\n1454\t              echo \"To intentionally allow a violation:\"\n1455\t              echo \"  1. Licenses: Add exception to deny.toml [licenses.exceptions]\"\n1456\t              echo \"  2. Bans: Add crate to deny.toml [bans.skip] or [bans.allow]\"\n1457\t              echo \"  3. Advisories: Add to deny.toml [advisories.ignore]\"\n1458\t              echo \"  4. For MPL/GPL exceptions: Create ADR in docs/adr/ first\"\n1459\t              echo \"\"\n1460\t              echo \"See: https://embarkstudios.github.io/cargo-deny/\"\n1461\t\n1462\t              exit 1\n1463\t            fi\n1464\t\n1465\t            # If we reach here, either all checks passed or there were only warnings\n1466\t            echo \"\"\n1467\t            echo \"=== All cargo-deny checks passed ===\"\n1468\t            echo \"Licenses: PASS\"\n1469\t            echo \"Bans: PASS (warnings allowed)\"\n1470\t            echo \"Sources: PASS\"\n1471\t            echo \"Advisories: PASS\"\n1472\t\n1473\t            # Count warnings for informational purposes\n1474\t            WARN_COUNT=$(echo \"$OUTPUT\" | grep -c \"warning\\[\" || echo \"0\")\n1475\t            if [ \"$WARN_COUNT\" -gt 0 ]; then\n1476\t              echo \"Note: $WARN_COUNT warning(s) present (non-blocking)\"\n1477\t            fi\n1478\t\n1479\t            # Generate JSON report for artifacts (optional, for record-keeping)\n1480\t            if command -v jq &> /dev/null; then\n1481\t              echo \"{\\\"status\\\":\\\"passed\\\",\\\"timestamp\\\":\\\"$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)\\\"}\" > /workspace/deny-report.json\n1482\t              echo \"Report generated: deny-report.json\"\n1483\t            fi\n1484\t        volumeMounts:\n1485\t          - name: workspace\n1486\t            mountPath: /workspace\n1487\t          - name: cargo-cache\n1488\t            mountPath: /cache/cargo\n1489\t        resources:\n1490\t          requests:\n1491\t            cpu: 500m\n1492\t            memory: 1Gi\n1493\t          limits:\n1494\t            cpu: 1000m\n1495\t            memory: 2Gi\n1496\t      outputs:\n1497\t        artifacts:\n1498\t          - name: deny-report\n1499\t            path: /workspace/deny-report.json\n1500\t            optional: true\n1501\t\n1502\t    # === Cargo Bloat ===\n1503\t    # Runs cargo-bloat to enforce the 4 MB binary size budget.\n1504\t    #\n1505\t    # This is a Tier 1 hard gate from Quality Targets. Binary size > 4 MB blocks\n1506\t    # PR merge. Without this gate, binary size regressions silently slip past code\n1507\t    # review and risk breaking the R2 target (single-page PDF extraction in < 100ms\n1508\t    # on a 1.6 GHz CPU, which requires a small binary to fit in CPU cache).\n1509\t    #\n1510\t    # Bead: pdftract-2rf\n1511\t    # Plan section: Phase 0.4 Quality Targets\n1512\t    #\n1513\t    # Enforcement policy:\n1514\t    # - Binary size (stripped) must be <= 4,194,304 bytes (4 MB) for x86_64-unknown-linux-musl\n1515\t    # - Other targets (macOS, Windows) are informational (not gated) due to larger metadata\n1516\t    # - Output is published as bloat-report.json artifact for historical tracking\n1517\t    # - A second invocation with --features remote tracks ureg contribution (PB-5 data)\n1518\t    #\n1519\t    # If budget exceeded, the first-line response is PB-2: switch wordlist to Bloom filter\n1520\t    # behind the wordlist-bloom feature (documented in ADR-002).\n1521\t    - name: cargo-bloat\n1522\t      activeDeadlineSeconds: 600\n1523\t      container:\n1524\t        image: ronaldraygun/pdftract-test-glibc:1.78\n1525\t        command: [bash, -c]\n1526\t        args:\n1527\t          - |\n1528\t            set -eo pipefail\n1529\t\n1530\t            echo \"==========================================\"\n1531\t            echo \"Cargo Bloat (Binary Size Budget)\"\n1532\t            echo \"==========================================\"\n1533\t\n1534\t            cd /workspace\n1535\t            export CARGO_HOME=\"/cache/cargo/registry\"\n1536\t            export CARGO_TARGET_DIR=\"/cache/cargo/target-bloat\"\n1537\t\n1538\t            # Install cargo-bloat if not present\n1539\t            if ! command -v cargo-bloat &> /dev/null; then\n1540\t              echo \"Installing cargo-bloat...\"\n1541\t              cargo install cargo-bloat --locked\n1542\t            fi\n1543\t\n1544\t            echo \"=== Running cargo bloat (default features, gated) ===\"\n1545\t            echo \"Target: x86_64-unknown-linux-musl\"\n1546\t            echo \"Budget: 4 MB (4,194,304 bytes)\"\n1547\t\n1548\t            # Build release binary first for accurate analysis\n1549\t            cargo build --release --target x86_64-unknown-linux-musl --features default --locked\n1550\t\n1551\t            # Run cargo bloat and capture output\n1552\t            cargo bloat --release --features default --crates --target x86_64-unknown-linux-musl -n 50 \\\n1553\t              > /tmp/bloat-default.txt 2>&1 || true\n1554\t\n1555\t            # Parse binary size from output\n1556\t            # cargo-bloat output format: \"File: pdftract X MB\"\n1557\t            BINARY_PATH=\"target/x86_64-unknown-linux-musl/release/pdftract\"\n1558\t            if [ ! -f \"$BINARY_PATH\" ]; then\n1559\t              echo \"ERROR: Binary not found at $BINARY_PATH\" >&2\n1560\t              exit 1\n1561\t            fi\n1562\t\n1563\t            # Get stripped binary size\n1564\t            STRIPPED_SIZE=$(x86_64-linux-musl-strip -o /tmp/pdftract-stripped \"$BINARY_PATH\" 2>/dev/null && stat -c%s /tmp/pdftract-stripped || stat -c%s \"$BINARY_PATH\")\n1565\t            BUDGET=4194304  # 4 MB\n1566\t\n1567\t            echo \"=== Binary size analysis ===\"\n1568\t            echo \"Stripped size: $STRIPPED_SIZE bytes\"\n1569\t            echo \"Budget: $BUDGET bytes\"\n1570\t            echo \"Remaining: $((BUDGET - STRIPPED_SIZE)) bytes\"\n1571\t\n1572\t            # Generate JSON report\n1573\t            cat > /workspace/bloat-report.json <<EOF\n1574\t            {\n1575\t              \"timestamp\": \"$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)\",\n1576\t              \"commit_sha\": \"{{workflow.parameters.commit-sha}}\",\n1577\t              \"target\": \"x86_64-unknown-linux-musl\",\n1578\t              \"features\": \"default\",\n1579\t              \"stripped_size_bytes\": $STRIPPED_SIZE,\n1580\t              \"budget_bytes\": $BUDGET,\n1581\t              \"within_budget\": $( [ \"$STRIPPED_SIZE\" -le \"$BUDGET\" ] && echo \"true\" || echo \"false\" ),\n1582\t              \"raw_output\": $(jq -R -s '.' < /tmp/bloat-default.txt)\n1583\t            }\n1584\t            EOF\n1585\t\n1586\t            # Check against budget\n1587\t            if [ \"$STRIPPED_SIZE\" -gt \"$BUDGET\" ]; then\n1588\t              echo \"==========================================\"\n1589\t              echo \"CARGO BLOAT CHECK FAILED\"\n1590\t              echo \"==========================================\"\n1591\t              echo \"Binary size exceeds 4 MB budget\"\n1592\t              echo \"Size: $STRIPPED_SIZE bytes\"\n1593\t              echo \"Budget: $BUDGET bytes\"\n1594\t              echo \"Over: $((STRIPPED_SIZE - BUDGET)) bytes\"\n1595\t              echo \"\"\n1596\t              echo \"First-line response (per PB-2):\"\n1597\t              echo \"  Switch wordlist to Bloom filter behind wordlist-bloom feature.\"\n1598\t              echo \"  See ADR-002 for implementation guidance.\"\n1599\t              echo \"\"\n1600\t              echo \"Top contributors:\"\n1601\t              head -30 /tmp/bloat-default.txt || true\n1602\t              echo \"\"\n1603\t              echo \"See bloat-report.json artifact for full details.\"\n1604\t              exit 1\n1605\t            fi\n1606\t\n1607\t            echo \"=== Running cargo bloat (remote features, informational) ===\"\n1608\t            echo \"This tracks ureg's contribution for PB-5 (alt-feature escape hatch)\"\n1609\t            cargo bloat --release --features remote --crates --target x86_64-unknown-linux-musl -n 50 \\\n1610\t              > /tmp/bloat-remote.txt 2>&1 || true\n1611\t\n1612\t            # Append remote feature data to report\n1613\t            if command -v jq &> /dev/null; then\n1614\t              jq --arg remote \"$(jq -R -s '.' < /tmp/bloat-remote.txt)\" \\\n1615\t                '. + {\"remote_features_raw\": $remote}' /workspace/bloat-report.json \\\n1616\t                > /tmp/bloat-report-merged.json && mv /tmp/bloat-report-merged.json /workspace/bloat-report.json\n1617\t            fi\n1618\t\n1619\t            echo \"=== Cargo bloat checks passed ===\"\n1620\t            echo \"Binary within 4 MB budget\"\n1621\t            echo \"Size: $STRIPPED_SIZE bytes ($(( STRIPPED_SIZE * 100 / BUDGET ))% of budget)\"\n1622\t\n1623\t            # Display top contributors for visibility\n1624\t            echo \"\"\n1625\t            echo \"Top 20 contributors:\"\n1626\t            head -30 /tmp/bloat-default.txt | tail -20 || true\n1627\t        volumeMounts:\n1628\t          - name: workspace\n1629\t            mountPath: /workspace\n1630\t          - name: cargo-cache\n1631\t            mountPath: /cache/cargo\n1632\t        resources:\n1633\t          requests:\n1634\t            cpu: 1000m\n1635\t            memory: 2Gi\n1636\t          limits:\n1637\t            cpu: 2000m\n1638\t            memory: 4Gi\n1639\t      outputs:\n1640\t        artifacts:\n1641\t          - name: bloat-report\n1642\t            path: /workspace/bloat-report.json\n1643\t\n1644\t    # === Memory Ceiling ===\n1645\t    # Runs memory ceiling tests to enforce RSS budgets.\n1646\t    #\n1647\t    # This is a Tier 1 hard gate from Quality Targets. Any document exceeding\n1648\t    # its memory budget blocks PR merge. Without this gate, memory regressions\n1649\t    # silently slip past code review and risk breaking the Memory targets.\n1650\t    #\n1651\t    # Bead: bf-1g1fd\n1652\t    # Plan section: Phase 0.4 Quality Targets - Memory targets\n1653\t    #\n1654\t    # Enforcement policy:\n1655\t    # - Peak RSS, 100-page vector PDF (buffered mode) < 512 MB\n1656\t    # - Peak RSS, streaming/NDJSON mode (any page count) < 256 MB\n1657\t    # - Peak RSS, adversarial fixtures < 1 GB hard ceiling\n1658\t    # - Output is published as memory-report.json artifact for historical tracking\n1659\t    # - Tests run under cgroup MemoryMax cap for clean failure mode\n1660\t    - name: memory-ceiling\n1661\t      activeDeadlineSeconds: 600\n1662\t      container:\n1663\t        image: ronaldraygun/pdftract-test-glibc:1.78\n1664\t        command: [bash, -c]\n1665\t        args:\n1666\t          - |\n1667\t            set -eo pipefail\n1668\t\n1669\t            echo \"==========================================\"\n1670\t            echo \"Memory Ceiling Tests\"\n1671\t            echo \"==========================================\"\n1672\t\n1673\t            cd /workspace\n1674\t            export CARGO_HOME=\"/cache/cargo/registry\"\n1675\t            export CARGO_TARGET_DIR=\"/cache/cargo/target-memory-ceiling\"\n1676\t\n1677\t            echo \"=== Running memory ceiling tests ===\"\n1678\t            echo \"Budgets:\"\n1679\t            echo \"  - Buffered 100-page: 512 MB\"\n1680\t            echo \"  - Streaming mode: 256 MB\"\n1681\t            echo \"  - Adversarial hard cap: 1024 MB\"\n1682\t            echo \"\"\n1683\t            echo \"Cgroup MemoryMax: 1536 MB (1.5 GB cap for clean failure)\"\n1684\t            echo \"  This enforces a hard ceiling on the entire test run.\"\n1685\t            echo \"  Individual document budgets are enforced by the harness.\"\n1686\t\n1687\t            # Check if cgroup v2 is available (preferred)\n1688\t            if [ -f /sys/fs/cgroup/cgroup.controllers ]; then\n1689\t              echo \"=== Using cgroup v2 for memory enforcement ===\"\n1690\t\n1691\t              # Enable memory controller\n1699\t              echo \"+memory\" > /sys/fs/cgroup/cgroup.subtree_control 2>/dev/null || true\n1700\t\n1701\t              # Launch the test in the cgroup\n1702\t              # - Generates memory-report.json with detailed results\n1706\t              (\n1707\t                # Add current process to the cgroup\n1708\t                echo $$ > \"$CGROUP_PATH/cgroup.procs\"\n1709\t\n1710\t                cd /workspace/xtask && cargo run --release -- memory-ceiling\n1711\t              ) || {\n1712\t                EXIT_CODE=$?\n1713\t\n1714\t                # Clean up cgroup\n1731\t              rmdir \"$CGROUP_PATH\" 2>/dev/null || true\n1732\t\n1733\t            elif [ -w /sys/fs/cgroup/memory/memory.limit_in_bytes ]; then\n1734\t              echo \"=== Using cgroup v1 for memory enforcement ===\"\n1735\t\n1736\t              # Clean up cgroup\n1776\t              rmdir \"$CGROUP_PATH\" 2>/dev/null || true\n1777\t\n1778\t            else\n1779\t              echo \"=== WARNING: No cgroup memory controller available ===\"\n1780\t              echo \"Running without cgroup MemoryMax enforcement.\"\n1781\t              echo \"Individual document budgets will still be enforced by the harness.\"\n1782\t              echo \"\"\n1783\t\n1784\t              # === Schema Generation Check ===\n1857\t    # Regenerates JSON schema from Rust types and verifies it matches the committed file.\n1858\t    #\n1859\t    # This is a Tier 1 hard gate from Phase 6.1.3. It ensures the auto-generated schema\n1860\t    # stays in sync with the Rust type definitions. Without this gate, schema drift\n1861\t    # silently slips past code review and the published schema becomes incorrect.\n1862\t    #\n1863\t    # Bead: pdftract-16h0a\n1864\t    # Plan section: Phase 6.1.3\n1865\t    #\n1866\t    # Enforcement policy:\n1867\t    # - Schema is regenerated via cargo xtask gen-schema\n1868\t    # - Regenerated output is compared to committed docs/schema/v1.0/pdftract.schema.json\n1869\t    # - Any diff (including whitespace, key order) fails the build\n1870\t    # - Checkout specific commit\n            echo \"=== Checking out commit ===\"\n            git checkout \"$COMMIT_SHA\"\n\n            # Get git info for metadata\n            GIT_REMOTE_URL=$(git remote get-url origin)\n            GIT_DESCRIBE=$(git describe --always --abbrev=40 --dirty 2>/dev/null || echo \"$COMMIT_SHA\")\n            GIT_AUTHOR=$(git log -1 --format='%an <%ae>' \"$COMMIT_SHA\" 2>/dev/null || echo \"unknown\")\n            GIT_COMMIT_MSG=$(git log -1 --format='%s' \"$COMMIT_SHA\" 2>/dev/null || echo \"unknown\")\n\n            echo \"Git remote: $GIT_REMOTE_URL\"\n            echo \"Git describe: $GIT_DESCRIBE\"\n            echo \"Git author: $GIT_AUTHOR\"\n            echo \"Git commit message: $GIT_COMMIT_MSG\"\n\n            # Get workflow template SHA (this file's commit)\n            PDFTRACT_CI_SHA=$(git log -1 --format='%H' .ci/argo-workflows/pdftract-ci.yaml 2>/dev/null || echo \"$COMMIT_SHA\")\n\n            # Record container image versions for reproducibility\n            CONTAINER_IMAGES='{\n              \"build:x86_64-unknown-linux-musl\": \"ghcr.io/cross-rs/x86_64-unknown-linux-musl:main\",\n              \"build:aarch64-unknown-linux-musl\": \"ghcr.io/cross-rs/aarch64-unknown-linux-musl:main\",\n              \"build:x86_64-apple-darwin\": \"ghcr.io/cross-rs/x86_64-apple-darwin:main\",\n              \"build:aarch64-apple-darwin\": \"ghcr.io/cross-rs/aarch64-apple-darwin:main\",\n              \"build:x86_64-pc-windows-gnu\": \"ghcr.io/cross-rs/x86_64-pc-windows-gnu:main\",\n              \"test:glibc\": \"rust:1.83-bookworm\",\n              \"test:musl\": \"ghcr.io/cross-rs/x86_64-unknown-linux-musl:main\",\n              \"quality\": \"ronaldraygun/pdftract-test-glibc:1.78\",\n              \"msrv\": \"rust:1.78-slim\",\n              \"bench\": \"python:3.11-slim-bookworm\"\n            }'\n\n            # Create workflow metadata artifact\n            echo \"=== Creating workflow metadata artifact ===\"\n            cat > /workspace/workflow-metadata.json <<EOF\n            {\n              \"commit_sha\": \"$COMMIT_SHA\",\n              \"ref\": \"$REF\",\n              \"git_remote_url\": \"$GIT_REMOTE_URL\",\n              \"git_describe\": \"$GIT_DESCRIBE\",\n              \"git_author\": \"$GIT_AUTHOR\",\n              \"git_commit_message\": $(echo \"$GIT_COMMIT_MSG\" | jq -Rs .),\n              \"pdftract_ci_template_sha\": \"$PDFTRACT_CI_SHA\",\n              \"workflow_parameters\": {\n                \"commit-sha\": \"$COMMIT_SHA\",\n                \"ref\": \"$REF\",\n                \"repo-url\": \"$REPO_URL\",\n                \"is-tag\": \"{{workflow.parameters.is-tag}}\",\n                \"regression-mode\":            \"proptest-cases\": \"{{workflow.parameters.proptest-cases}}\"\n              },\n              \"container_images\": $CONTAINER_IMAGES,\n              \"workflow_name\": \"{{workflow.name}}\",\n              \"argo_ui_url\": \"https://argo-ci.ardenone.com/workflows/argo-workflows/{{workflow.name}}\",\n              \"timestamp\": \"$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)\"\n            }\n            EOF\n\n            echo \"=== Workflow metadata ===\"\n            cat /workspace/workflow-metadata.json | jq '.'\n\n            echo \"\"\n            echo \"=== Repository ready ===\"\n            echo \"Workspace: /workspace\"\n            echo \"Cargo cache: /cache/cargo\"\n        volumeMounts:\n          - name: workspace\n            mountPath: /workspace\n          - name: cargo-cache\n            mountPath: /cache/cargo\n        resources:\n          requests:\n            cpu: 500m\n            memory: 1Gi\n          limits:\n            cpu: 1000m\n            memory: 2Gi\n      outputs:\n        artifacts:\n          - name: workflow-metadata\n            path: /workspace/workflow-metadata.json\n\n    # === Build Matrix ===\n    # Cross-compile for 5 targets using cross (Docker-based)\n    # Targets: x86_64-unknown-linux-musl, aarch64-unknown-linux-musl,\n    #          x86_64-apple-darwin, aarch64-apple-darwin, x86_64-pc-windows-gnu\n    - name: build-matrix\n      activeDeadlineSeconds: 3600\n      dag:\n        onExit: build-matrix-exit\n        tasks:\n          - name: build-linux-x86_64-musl\n            template: build-target\n            arguments:\n              parameters:\n                - name: target\n                  value: \"x86_64-unknown-linux-musl\"\n                - name: cross-image\n                  value: \"ghcr.io/cross-rs/x86_64-unknown-linux-musl:main\"\n                - name: strip-cmd\n                  value: \"x86_64-linux-musl-strip\"\n                - name: ext\n                  value: \"\"\n            continueOn:\n              failed: true\n          - name: build-linux-aarch64-musl\n            template: build-target\n            arguments:\n              parameters:\n                - name: target\n                  value: \"aarch64-unknown-linux-musl\"\n                - name: cross-image\n                  value: \"ghcr.io/cross-rs/aarch64-unknown-linux-musl:main\"\n      - name: build-darwin-x86_64\n            template: build-target\n            arguments:\n              parameters:\n                - name: target\n                  value: \"x86_64-apple-darwin\"\n                - name: cross-image\n                  value: \"ghcr.io/cross-rs/x86_64-apple-darwin:main\"\n                - name: strip-cmd\n                  value: \"x86_64-apple-darwin-strip\"\n                - name: ext\n                  value: \"\"\n            continueOn:\n              failed: true\n          - name: build-darwin-aarch64\n            template: build-target\n            arguments:\n              parameters:\n                - name: target\n                  value: \"aarch64-apple-darwin\"\n                - name: cross-image\n                  value: \"ghcr.io/cross-rs/aarch64-apple-darwin:main\"\n                - name: strip-cmd\n                  value: \"aarch64-apple-darwin-strip\"\n                - name: ext\n                  value: \"\"\n            continueOn:\n              failed: true\n          - name: build-windows-x86_64-gnu\n            template: build-target\n            arguments:\n \".exe\"\n            continueOn:\n              failed: true\n\n    # === Build Target Template ===\n    # Single target build using cross (Docker-based)\n    # Uses ghcr.io/cross-rs/<target>:main images which have cross pre-installed\n    - name: build-target\n      inputs:\n        parameters:\n          - name: target\n          - name: cross-image\n          - name: strip-cmd\n          - name: ext\n      activeDeadlineSeconds: 3600\n      container:\n        image: \"{{inputs.parameters.cross-image}}\"\n        command: [bash, -c]\n        args:\n          - |\n            set -eo pipefail\n\n            TARGET=\"{{inputs.parameters.target}}\"\n            STRIP_CMD=\"{{inputs.parameters.strip-cmd}}\"\n            EXT=\"{{inputs.parameters.ext}}\"\n\n            echo \"==========================================\"\n            echo \"Building pdftract for target: $TARGET\"\n            echo \"==========================================\"\n\n            cd /workspace\n\n            # Set reproducible build timestamp\n            export SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=$(git log -1 --format=%ct 2>/dev/null || echo 0)\n            export CARGO_HOME=\"/cache/cargo/registry\"\n            export CARGO_TARGET_DIR=\"/cache/cargo/target-$TARGET\"\n\n            echo \"SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=$SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH\"\n            echo \"CARGO_HOME=$CARGO_HOME\"\n            echo \"CARGO_TARGET_DIR=$CARGO_TARGET_DIR\"\n\n            echo \"=== Running cargo build with cross ===\"\n            cross build --release --target \"$TARGET\" --locked --features default,serve,decrypt\n\n            BINARY_PATH=\"target/$TARGET/release/pdftract$EXT\"\n\n            if [ ! -f \"$BINARY_PATH\" ]; then\n              echo \"ERROR: Binary not found at $BINARY_PATH\" >&2\n              echo \"Contents of target directory:\"\n              ls -la \"target/$TARGET/release/\" || true\n              exit 1\n            fi\n\n            echo \"=== Binary size before strip ===\"\n            ls -lh \"$BINARY_PATH\"\n\n            echo \"=== Stripping binary ===\"\n            \"$STRIP_CMD\" \"$BINARY_PATH\" || {\n              echo \"WARNING: Strip command failed, continuing with unstripped binary\" >&2\n            }\n\n            echo \"=== Binary size after strip ===\"\n            ls -lh \"$BINARY_PATH\"\n\n            mkdir -p /artifacts\n            cp \"$BINARY_PATH\" \"/artifacts/pdftract-$TARGET$EXT\"\n\n            echo \"=== Final artifact ===\"\n            ls -lh /artifacts/\n\n            SIZE=$(stat -c%s \"/artifacts/pdftract-$TARGET$EXT\" 2>/dev/null || stat -f%z \"/artifacts/pdftract-$TARGET$EXT\")\n            echo \"Binary size: $SIZE bytes\"\n\n            if [ \"$SIZE\" -gt 4194304 ]; then\n              echo \"WARNING: Binary exceeds 4 MB budget ($SIZE bytes)\"\n            else\n              echo \"Binary within 4 MB budget\"\n            fi\n\n            echo \"=== Build complete ===\"\n        volumeMounts:\n          - name: workspace\n            mountPath: /workspace\n          - name: cargo-cache\n            mountPath: /cache/cargo\n          - name: docker-config\n            mountPath: /root/.docker\n        resources:\n          requests:\n            cpu: 2000m\n            memory: 4Gi\n          limits:\n            cpu: 4000m\n            memory: 8Gi\n      outputs:\n        artifacts:\n          - name: pdftract-binary\n            path: /artifacts/pdftract-{{inputs.parameters.target}}{{inputs.parameters.ext}}\n\n    # === Build Matrix Exit Handler ===\n    - name: build-matrix-exit\n      script:\n        image: alpine:3.19\n        command: [sh]\n        source: |\n          #!/bin/sh\n          echo \"=== Build Matrix Exit Report ===\"\n          echo \"Commit: {{workflow.parameters.commit-sha}}\"\n          echo \"All binaries available as artifacts\"\n\n    # === Test Matrix ===\n    # Run cargo test across feature combinations and targets\n    # - glibc: All features including OCR (tesseract available on Debian)\n    # - musl: Production binary feature set (no OCR, unavailable on Alpine/musl)\n    # - proptest: Property tests (10,000 cases per module)\n    #\n    # CRITICAL: All cargo commands MUST use --locked (or --locked --frozen)\n    #\n    # Bead: pdftract-5gtcj (musl leg)\n    # Plan section: Phase 0.3\n    - name: test-matrix\n      activeDeadlineSeconds: 3600\n      dag:\n        tasks:\n          - name: test-glibc\n            template: test-glibc\n          - name: test-musl\n            template: test-musl\n\n    # === Test GLIBC ===\n    # Run full test suite on x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu with all features including OCR\n    # Uses standard Debian-based Rust image with tesseract available\n    #\n    # Features tested: default, all (including ocr, serve, decrypt, python)\n    #\n    # Memory enforcement (bf-1g1fd):\n    # - Cgroup MemoryMax: 6 GB (hard ceiling on entire test run)\n    # This ensures clean failure mode for memory regressions in tests.\n    - name: test-glibc\n      activeDeadlineSeconds: 3600\n      container:\n        image: rust:1.83-bookworm\n        command: [bash, -c]\n        args:\n          - |\n            set -eo pipefail\n\n            echo \"==========================================\"\n            echo \"Test GLIBC (x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu)\"\n            echo \"==========================================\"\n\n            cd /workspace\n            export CARGO_HOME=\"/cache/cargo/registry\"\n            export CARGO_TARGET_DIR=\"/cache/cargo/target-test-glibc\"\n            MEMORY_MAX_MB=6144  # 6 GB cgroup cap for test suite\n\n            # Check if cgroup v2 is available (preferred)\n            if [ -f /sys/fs/cgroup/cgroup.controllers ]; then\n              echo \"=== Using cgroup v2 for memory enforcement (bf-1g1fd) ===\"\n\n              # Create a child cgroup for this test run\n              CGROUP_PATH=\"/sys/fs/cgroup/test-glibc\"\n              mkdir -p \"$CGROUP_PATH\"\n\n              # Set memory limit\n              echo \"max ${MEMORY_MAX_MB}M\" > \"$CGROUP_PATH/memory.max\"\n\n              # Enable memory controller\n              echo \"+memory\" > /sys/fs/cgroup/cgroup.subtree_control 2>/dev/null || true\n\n              # Launch the tests in the cgroup\n              (\n                # Add current process to the cgroup\n                echo $$ > \"$CGROUP_PATH/cgroup.procs\"\n\n                # Set proptest seed for reproducibility\n                SEED=\"{{workflow.parameters.proptest-seed}}\"\n                if [ -z \"$SEED\" ]; then\n                  SEED=$(date +%s%N | sha256sum | head -c 16)\n                  echo \"Generated proptest seed: $SEED\"\n                else\n                  echo \"Using provided proptest seed: $SEED\"\n                fi\n                export PROPTEST_SEED=\"$SEED\"\n\n                # Set proptest case count\n                CASES=\"{{workflow.parameters.proptest-cases}}\"\n                echo \"Proptest cases per module: $CASES\"\n                export PROPTEST_CASES=\"$CASES\"\n\n                echo \"=== Running unit tests (default features) ===\"\n                cargo test --locked --lib --bins\n\n                echo \"=== Running unit tests (all features including OCR) ===\"\n                cargo test --locked --all-features --lib --bins\n\n                echo \"=== Running property tests (proptest) ===\"\n                echo \"Seed: $PROPTEST_SEED | Cases: $PROPTEST_CASES\"\n                cargo nextest run --features proptest --proptest --profile=ci-proptest || {\n                  EXIT_CODE=$?\n                  if [ $EXIT_CODE -ne 0 ]; then\n                    echo \"ERROR: Property tests failed!\"\n                    echo \"Check proptest-regressions/ for new minimal counterexamples\"\n                    exit $EXIT_CODE\n                  fi\n                }\n\n                echo \"=== All glibc tests passed ===\"\n                echo \"Unit tests: PASS\"\n                echo \"Property tests: PASS ($CASES cases per module)\"\n              ) || {\n                EXIT_CODE=$?\n                # Clean up cgroup\n                rmdir \"$CGROUP_PATH\" 2>/dev/null || true\n                exit $EXIT_CODE\n              }\n\n              # Clean up cgroup\n              rmdir \"$CGROUP_PATH\" 2>/dev/null || true\n\n            elif [ -w /sys/fs/cgroup/memory/memory.limit_in_bytes ]; then\n              echo \"=== Using cgroup v1 for memory enforcement (bf-1g1fd) ===\"\n\n              # Create a cgroup for this test run (cgroup v1)\n              CGROUP_PATH=\"/sys/fs/cgroup/memory/test-glibc\"\n\n              # Clean up any existing cgroup\n              mkdir -p \"$CGROUP_PATH\" 2>/dev/null || rmdir \"$CGROUP_PATH\" 2>/dev/null\n              mkdir -p \"$CGROUP_PATH\"\n\n              # Set memory limit\n          echo 0 > \"$CGROUP_PATH/memory.oom_control\" 2>/dev/null || true\n\n              # Launch the tests in the cgroup\n              (\n                # Add current process to the cgroup\n                echo $$ > \"$CGROUP_PATH/tasks\"\n\n                # Set proptest seed for reproducibility\n                SEED=\"{{workflow.parameters.proptest-seed}}\"\n                if [ -z \"$SEED\" ]; then\n                  SEED=$(date +%s%N | sha256sum | head -c 16)\n                  echo \"Generated proptest seed: $SEED\"\n                else\n                  echo \"Using provided proptest seed: $SEED\"\n                fi\n                export PROPTEST_SEED=\"$SEED\"\n\n                # Set proptest case count\n                CASES=\"{{workflow.parameters.proptest-cases}}\"\n                echo \"Proptest cases per module: $CASES\"\n                export PROPTEST_CASES=\"$CASES\"\n\n                echo \"=== Running --locked --all-features --lib --bins\n\n                echo \"=== Running property tests (proptest) ===\"\n                echo \"Seed: $PROPTEST_SEED | Cases: $PROPTEST_CASES\"\n        rmdir \"$CGROUP_PATH\" 2>/dev/null || true\n\n            else\n              echo \"=== WARNING: No cgroup memory controller available ===\"\n              echo \"Running without cgroup MemoryMax enforcement (bf-1g1fd)\"\n              echo \"\"\n\n              # Set proptest seed for reproducibility\n              SEED=\"{{workflow.parameters.proptest-seed}}\"\n              if [        exit $EXIT_CODE\n                }\n\n                echo \"=== Converting test output to JUnit XML ===\"\n                if command -v jq &> /dev/null; then\n                  # Convert == \"test\") |\n                    \"<testcase name=\\(.name | @sh) classname=\\(.crate | @sh) time=\\(.exec_time // 0)>\" +\n                    if .status == \"ok\" then\n                      \"</testcase>\"\n                    else\n                      \"<failure message=\\(.message | @sh)>\\(.stdout // \"\" | @sh)</failure></testcase>\"\n                    end\n                  ' /tmp/test-output.json > /workspace/test-results-musl.xml || {\n                    echo \"WARN: JUnit XML generation failed, creating minimal report\"\n                    echo '<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?><testsuites name=\"musl\"><testsuite tests=\"1\"><testcase name=\"musl-tests\" classname=\"pdftract\"/></testsuite></testsuites>' > /workspace/test-results-musl.xml\n                  }\n                else\n                  echo '<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?><testsuites name=\"musl\"><testsuite tests=\"1\"><testcase name=\"musl-tests\" rmdir \"$CGROUP_PATH\" 2>/dev/null || true\n                exit $EXIT_CODE\n              }\n\n              # Clean up cgroup\n              rmdir \"$CGROUP_PATH\" 2>/dev/null || true\n\n \"=== Installing cross ===\"\n                if ! command -v cross &> /dev/null; then\n                  echo \"cross not found in image, installing...\"\n                  cargo install --locked cross || {\n                    echo \"ERROR: Failed to install cross\" >&2\n                    exit 1\n                  }\n                fi\n                cross --version || echo \"cross             echo \"Test threads: 4\"\n\n                cross test --release --target x86_64-unknown-linux-musl \\\n                  --features default,serve,decrypt \\\n                  --locked -- \\\n                  --test-threads=4 \\\n                  -Z unstable-options \\\n                  --format json \\\n                  2>&1 | tee /tmp/test-output.json || {\n                  EXIT_CODE=$?\n                  echo \"ERROR: musl tests failed with exit code $EXIT_CODE\"\n                  cat /tmp/test-output.json\n                  exit $EXIT_CODE\n                }\n\n                echo \"=== Converting test output to JUnit XML ===\"\n                if command -v jq &> /dev/null; then\n                  # Convert cargo test JSON output to JUnit XML format\n                  # This is a simplified conversion - for full JUnit support, use cargo-nextest\n                  jq -r '\n                    select(.type == \"test\") |\n                    \"<testcase name=\\(.name | @sh) classname=\\(.crate | @sh) time=\\(.exec_time // 0)>\" +\n                    if .status == \"ok\" then\n                      \"</testcase>\"\n                    else\n                      \"<failure message=\\(.message | @sh)>\\(.stdout // \"\" | @sh)</failure></testcase>\"\n                    end\n                  ' /tmp/test-output.json > /workspace/test-results-musl.xml || {\n                    echo \"WARN: JUnit XML generation failed, creating minimal report\"\n                    echo '<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?><testsuites name=\"musl\"><testsuite tests=\"1\"><testcase name=\"musl-tests\" classname=\"pdftract\"/></testsuite></testsuites>' > /workspace/test-results-musl.xml\n                  }\n                else\n                  echo '<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?><testsuites name=\"musl\"><testsuite tests=\"1\"><testcase name=\"musl-tests\" classname=\"pdftract\"/></testsuite></testsuites>' > /workspace/test-results-musl.xml\n                fi\n\n                echo \"=== All musl tests passed ===\"\n                echo \"Feature set: default,serve,decrypt (no OCR)\"\n                echo \"JUnit XML: test-results-musl.xml\"\n              ) || {\n                EXIT_CODE=$?\n                # Clean up cgroup\n                rmdir \"$CGROUP_PATH\" 2>/dev/null || true\n                exit $EXIT_CODE\n              }\n\n              # Clean up cgroup\n              rmdir \"$CGROUP_PATH\" 2>/dev/null || true\n\n            else\n              echo \"=== WARNING: No cgroup memory controller available ===\"\n              echo \"Running without cgroup MemoryMax enforcement (bf-1g1fd)\"\n              echo \"\"\n\n              echo \"=== Installing cross ===\"\n              if ! command -v cross &> /dev/null; then\n                echo \"cross not found in image, installing...\"\n                cargo install --locked cross || {\n                  echo \"ERROR: Failed to install cross\" >&2\n                  exit 1\n                }\n              fi\n              cross --version || echo \"cross version check failed\"\n\n              echo \"=== Running musl tests (features: default,serve,decrypt) ===\"\n              echo \"Note: OCR excluded (tesseract unavailable on Alpine/musl)\"\n              echo \"Test threads: 4\"\n\n              cross test --release --target x86_64-unknown-linux-musl \\\n                --features default,serve,decrypt \\\n                --locked -- \\\n                --test-threads=4 \\\n                -Z unstable-options \\\n                --format json \\\n                2>&1 | tee /tmp/test-output.json || {\n                EXIT_CODE=$?\n                echo \"ERROR: musl tests failed with exit code $EXIT_CODE\"\n                cat /tmp/test-output.json\n                exit $EXIT_CODE\n              }\n\n              echo \"=== Converting test output to JUnit XML ===\"\n              if command -v jq &> /dev/null; then\n                # Convert cargo test JSON output to JUnit XML format\n                # This is a simplified conversion - for full JUnit support, use cargo-nextest\n                jq -r '\n                  select(.type == \"test\") |\n                  \"<testcase name=\\(.name | @sh) classname=\\(.crate | @sh) time=\\(.exec_time // 0)>\" +\n                  if .status == \"ok\" then\n                    \"</testcase>\"\n                  else\n                    \"<failure message=\\(.message | @sh)>\\(.stdout // \"\" | @sh)</failure></testcase>\"\n                  end\n                ' /tmp/test-output.json > /workspace/test-results-musl.xml || {\n                  echo \"WARN: JUnit XML generation failed, creating minimal report\"\n                  echo '<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?><testsuites name=\"musl\"><testsuite tests=\"1\"><testcase name=\"musl-tests\" classname=\"pdftract\"/></testsuite></testsuites>' > /workspace/test-results-musl.xml\n                }\n              else\n                echo '<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?><testsuites name=\"musl\"><testsuite tests=\"1\"><testcase name=\"musl-tests\" classname=\"pdftract\"/></testsuite></testsuites>' > /workspace/test-results-musl.xml\n              fi\n\n              echo \"=== All musl tests passed ===\"\n              echo \"Feature set: default,serve,decrypt (no OCR)\"\n              echo \"JUnit XML: test-results-musl.xml\"\n            fi\n        volumeMounts:\n          - name: workspace\n            mountPath: /workspace\n          - name: cargo-cache\n            mountPath: /cache/cargo\n          - name: docker-config\n            mountPath: /root/.docker\n        resources:\n          requests:\n            cpu: 2000m\n            memory: 4Gi\n          limits:\n            cpu: 4000m\n            memory: 8Gi\n      outputs:\n        artifacts:\n          - name: test-results-musl\n            path: /workspace/test-results-musl.xml\n\n    # === Quality Matrix ===\n    # Run linting (clippy, fmt), security audit (cargo-audit), dependency review,\n    # license/ban/advisory checks (cargo-deny), MSRV check, binary size budget,\n    # memory ceiling enforcement, schema generation consistency, and log policy enforcement.\n    #\n    # Eight parallel Tier 1 quality gates — any failure blocks PR merge:\n    # 1. clippy-fmt: General linting and formatting check with INV-8 unwrap/expect ban\n    # 2. msrv-check: Verify no newer Rust features are used (MSRV 1.78)\n    # 3. cargo-audit: Security advisory check on dependencies\n    # 4. cargo-deny: License and security policy enforcement\n    # 5. cargo-bloat: Binary size budget enforcement (<= 4 MB)\n    # 6. memory-ceiling: Memory budget enforcement (analogous to cargo-bloat for RSS)\n    # 7. log-policy-check: NEVER-log secrets enforcement (grep-based scan)\n    # 8. schema-gen: Schema generation consistency check\n    #\n    # CRITICAL: All cargo commands MUST use --locked (or --locked --frozen)\n    - name: quality-matrix\n      activeDeadlineSeconds: 900\n      dag:\n        tasks:\n          - name: clippy-fmt\n            template: clippy-fmt\n          - name: msrv-check\n            template: msrv-check\n          - name: cargo-audit\n            template: cargo-audit\n          - name: cargo-deny\n            template: cargo-deny\n          - name: cargo-bloat\n            template: cargo-bloat\n          - name: memory-ceiling\n            template: memory-ceiling\n          - name: log-policy-check\n            template: log-policy-check\n          - name: schema-gen\n            template: schema-gen\n\n    # === Clippy and Fmt Check ===\n    # Runs clippy with warnings denied and INV-8 unwrap/expect enforcement.\n    # This is a Tier 1 hard gate: any single failure blocks PR merge.\n    #\n    # Bead: pdftract-3cp3a\n    # Plan section: Phase 0.4 Quality Targets\n    #\n    # Two-pass clippy strategy:\n    # 1. Full workspace check with --features default,serve,decrypt and -D warnings\n    # 2. Library-only check with -D clippy::unwrap_used -D clippy::expect_used (INV-8)\n    #    The unwrap/expect ban applies ONLY to pdftract-core library code; test code\n    #    and binaries retain permissive defaults.\n    - name: clippy-fmt\n      activeDeadlineSeconds: 900\n      container:\n        image: ronaldraygun/pdftract-test-glibc:1.78\n        command: [bash, -c]\n        args:\n          - |\n            set -eo pipefail\n\n            echo \"==========================================\"\n            echo \"Clippy and Format Check\"\n            echo \"==========================================\"\n\n            cd /workspace\n            export CARGO_HOME=\"/cache/cargo/registry\"\n            export CARGO_TARGET_DIR=\"/cache/cargo/target-clippy\"\n\n            echo \"=== Running clippy (full workspace) ===\"\n            echo \"Features: default,serve,decrypt\"\n            cargo clippy --locked --all-targets --features default,serve,decrypt -- -D warnings\n\n            echo \"=== Running clippy (library-only INV-8 check) ===\"\n            echo \"Enforcing: no unwrap() or expect() in pdftract-core\"\n            cargo clippy --locked --lib --features default,serve,decrypt \\\n              -- -D warnings -D clippy::unwrap_used -D clippy::expect_used\n\n            echo \"=== Running fmt check ===\"\n            cargo fmt --check\n\n            echo \"=== Clippy and fmt checks passed ===\"\n        volumeMounts:\n          - name: workspace\n            mountPath: /workspace\n          - name: cargo-cache\n            mountPath: /cache/cargo\n        resources:\n          requests:\n            cpu: 1000m\n            memory: 2Gi\n          limits:\n            cpu: 2000m\n            memory: 4Gi\n\n    # === MSRV Check ===\n    # Builds with rust:1.78-slim to verify no newer Rust features are used.\n    # This gate prevents silent MSRV drift that would break downstream consumers\n    # on older toolchains.\n    #\n    # Bead: pdftract-2ai37\n    # Plan section: Phase 0.4 Quality Targets\n    - name: msrv-check\n      activeDeadlineSeconds: 600\n      container:\n        image: rust:1.78-slim\n        command: [bash, -c]\n        args:\n          - |\n            set -eo pipefail\n\n            echo \"==========================================\"\n            echo \"MSRV Check (Rust 1.78)\"\n            echo \"==========================================\"\n\n            cd /workspace\n            export CARGO_HOME=\"/cache/cargo/registry\"\n            export CARGO_TARGET_DIR=\"/cache/cargo/target-msrv\"\n\n            echo \"=== Building with Rust 1.78 (MSRV) ===\"\n            rustc --version\n\n            # Build workspace with default features to catch MSRV violations\n            cargo build --workspace --features default --locked\n\n            echo \"=== MSRV check passed ===\"\n            echo \"No Rust 1.79+ features detected\"\n        volumeMounts:\n          - name: workspace\n            mountPath: /workspace\n          - name: cargo-cache\n            mountPath: /cache/cargo\n        resources:\n          requests:\n            cpu: 1000m\n            memory: 2Gi\n          limits:\n            cpu: 2000m\n            memory: 4Gi\n\n    # === Cargo Audit ===\n    # Runs cargo-audit to check for security vulnerabilities in dependencies\n    #\n    # This is a Tier 1 hard gate from Quality Targets. Any single gate failure\n    # blocks PR merge. Without it, this class of regression silently slips past\n    # code review.\n    #\n    # Bead: pdftract-5gs4p\n    # Plan section: Phase 0.4 Quality Targets\n    #\n    # Severity gating policy:\n    # - Warnings are denied (non-zero exit code on any warning)\n    # - >= medium severity advisories block PR merge\n    # - Unmaintained advisories are ignored (informational only)\n    # - audit.toml maintains allow-list of intentionally-ignored advisories\n    - name: cargo-audit\n      activeDeadlineSeconds: 300\n      container:\n        image: ronaldraygun/pdftract-test-glibc:1.78\n        command: [bash, -c]\n        args:\n          - |\n            set -eo pipefail\n\n            echo \"==========================================\"\n            echo \"Security Audit (cargo-audit)\"\n            echo \"==========================================\"\n\n            cd /workspace\n            export CARGO_HOME=\"/cache/cargo/registry\"\n\n            # Install cargo-audit if not present\n            if ! command -v cargo-audit &> /dev/null; then\n              echo \"Installing cargo-audit...\"\n              cargo install cargo-audit --locked\n            fi\n\n            echo \"=== Running cargo audit with severity gating ===\"\n            echo \"Policy: deny warnings, block on >= medium severity, ignore unmaintained\"\n            echo \"Configuration: audit.toml (allow-list for ignored advisories)\"\n\n            # Run audit with severity gating\n            # --deny warnings: fail on any warning\n            # --ignore unmaintained: ignore unmaintained crate warnings\n            # --severity: report only >= medium severity (low is informational)\n            # --json: output both JSON (for artifacts) and human-readable (for logs)\n            cargo audit --locked --deny warnings --ignore unmaintained \\\n              --severity medium \\\n              --json > /tmp/audit-report.json \\\n              || {\n                EXIT_CODE=$?\n\n                # Human-readable error summary for PR comments\n                echo \"==========================================\"\n                echo \"SECURITY AUDIT FAILED\"\n                echo \"==========================================\"\n\n                # Parse and display vulnerabilities from JSON\n                if command -v jq &> /dev/null; then\n                  VULN_COUNT=$(jq -r '.vulnerabilities.count // 0' /tmp/audit-report.json 2>/dev/null || echo \"0\")\n                  WARNING_COUNT=$(jq -r '.warnings | length // 0' /tmp/audit-report.json 2>/dev/null || echo \"0\")\n\n                  echo \"Vulnerabilities: $VULN_COUNT\"\n                  echo \"Warnings: $WARNING_COUNT\"\n\n                  if [ \"$VULN_COUNT\" -gt 0 ]; then\n                    echo \"\"\n                    echo \"Affected dependencies:\"\n                    jq -r '.vulnerabilities.list[]? | \"\\(.advisory.id) - \\(.package.name)@\\(.package.version): \\(.advisory.title)\"' \\\n                      /tmp/audit-report.json 2>/dev/null || true\n                  fi\n                fi\n\n                echo \"\"\n                echo \"Check the audit-report.json artifact for full details.\"\n                echo \"To intentionally ignore an advisory, add it to audit.toml with justification.\"\n\n                exit $EXIT_CODE\n              }\n\n            # Parse and display summary for CI logs\n            if command -v jq &> /dev/null; then\n              VULN_COUNT=$(jq -r '.vulnerabilities.count // 0' /tmp/audit-report.json 2>/dev/null || echo \"0\")\n              DEP_COUNT=$(jq -r '.lockfile.dependency-count // 0' /tmp/audit-report.json 2>/dev/null || echo \"0\")\n\n              echo \"=== Security audit passed ===\"\n              echo \"Dependencies scanned: $DEP_COUNT\"\n              echo \"Vulnerabilities found: $VULN_COUNT\"\n              echo \"Severity threshold: >= medium (denied)\"\n            else\n              echo \"=== Security audit passed ===\"\n            fi\n\n            # Copy report to workspace for artifact upload\n            cp /tmp/audit-report.json /workspace/audit-report.json\n        volumeMounts:\n          - name: workspace\n            mountPath: /workspace\n          - name: cargo-cache\n            mountPath: /cache/cargo\n        resources:\n          requests:\n            cpu: 500m\n            memory: 1Gi\n          limits:\n            cpu: 1000m\n            memory: 2Gi\n      outputs:\n        artifacts:\n          - name: audit-report\n            path: /workspace/audit-report.json\n\n    # === Cargo Deny ===\n    # Runs cargo-deny to check licenses, bans, sources, and advisories\n    #\n    # This is a Tier 1 hard gate from Quality Targets. Any single gate failure\n    # blocks PR merge. Without it, license violations, banned dependencies, or\n    # source registry issues silently slip past code review.\n    #\n    # Bead: pdftract-1rljr\n    # Plan section: Phase 0.4 Quality Targets\n    #\n    # Enforcement policy:\n    # - Licenses: Only MIT, Apache-2.0, BSD-2/3-Clause, ISC, Zlib, Unicode-DFS-2016 allowed\n    # - GPL/AGPL/LGPL are denied (copyleft contamination)\n    # - MPL-2.0 exceptions require ADR documentation (cbindgen, option-ext)\n    # - Bans: Wildcard dependencies denied, duplicate versions warned\n    # - Advisories: Yanked crates denied, RustSec advisories denied (with exceptions)\n    # - Sources: Unknown registries and git sources denied\n    # - deny.toml maintains the policy configuration and exceptions\n    - name: cargo-deny\n      activeDeadlineSeconds: 300\n      container:\n        image: ronaldraygun/pdftract-test-glibc:1.78\n        command: [bash, -c]\n        args:\n          - |\n            set -eo pipefail\n\n            echo \"==========================================\"\n            echo \"License, Ban, Source, Advisory Check (cargo-deny)\"\n            echo \"==========================================\"\n\n            cd /workspace\n            export CARGO_HOME=\"/cache/cargo/registry\"\n\n            # Install cargo-deny if not present\n            if ! command -v cargo-deny &> /dev/null; then\n              echo \"Installing cargo-deny...\"\n              cargo install cargo-deny --locked\n            fi\n\n            echo \"=== Running cargo deny check ===\"\n            echo \"Checks: licenses, bans, sources, advisories\"\n            echo \"Configuration: deny.toml (policy and exceptions)\"\n\n            # Run all checks in one command\n            # Note: cargo-deny returns exit code 1 for warnings and 2 for errors/denials\n            # We treat warnings (duplicate versions) as PASS, actual denials as FAIL\n            OUTPUT=$(cargo deny check \\\n              licenses bans sources advisories \\\n              2>&1) || EXIT_CODE=$?\n\n            echo \"$OUTPUT\"\n\n            # Parse output to determine if there are actual denials (not just warnings)\n            # Denials contain \"error[\" prefix, warnings contain \"warning[\" prefix\n            if echo \"$OUTPUT\" | grep -q \"error\\[\"; then\n              echo \"==========================================\"\n              echo \"CARGO DENY CHECKS FAILED\"\n              echo \"==========================================\"\n\n              echo \"\"\n              echo \"One or more checks were denied:\"\n              echo \"  - licenses: Dependency license violations\"\n              echo \"  - bans: Banned crates (not duplicate version warnings)\"\n              echo \"  - sources: Unknown registries or git sources\"\n              echo \"  - advisories: Security vulnerabilities (RustSec)\"\n              echo \"\"\n              echo \"Review the error output above for specific violations.\"\n              echo \"To intentionally allow a violation:\"\n              echo \"  1. Licenses: Add exception to deny.toml [licenses.exceptions]\"\n              echo \"  2. Bans: Add crate to deny.toml [bans.skip] or [bans.allow]\"\n              echo \"  3. Advisories: Add to deny.toml [advisories.ignore]\"\n              echo \"  4. For MPL/GPL exceptions: Create ADR in docs/adr/ first\"\n              echo \"\"\n              echo \"See: https://embarkstudios.github.io/cargo-deny/\"\n\n              exit 1\n            fi\n\n            # If we reach here, either all checks passed or there were only warnings\n            echo \"\"\n            echo \"=== All cargo-deny checks passed ===\"\n            echo \"Licenses: PASS\"\n            echo \"Bans: PASS (warnings allowed)\"\n            echo \"Sources: PASS\"\n            echo \"Advisories: PASS\"\n\n            # Count warnings for informational purposes\n            WARN_COUNT=$(echo \"$OUTPUT\" | grep -c \"warning\\[\" || echo \"0\")\n            if [ \"$WARN_COUNT\" -gt 0 ]; then\n              echo \"Note: $WARN_COUNT warning(s) present (non-blocking)\"\n            fi\n\n            # Generate JSON report for artifacts (optional, for record-keeping)\n            if command -v jq &> /dev/null; then\n              echo \"{\\\"status\\\":\\\"passed\\\",\\\"timestamp\\\":\\\"$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)\\\"}\" > /workspace/deny-report.json\n              echo \"Report generated: deny-report.json\"\n            fi\n        volumeMounts:\n          - name: workspace\n            mountPath: /workspace\n          - name: cargo-cache\n            mountPath: /cache/cargo\n        resources:\n          requests:\n            cpu: 500m\n            memory: 1Gi\n          limits:\n            cpu: 1000m\n            memory: 2Gi\n      outputs:\n        artifacts:\n          - name: deny-report\n            path: /workspace/deny-report.json\n            optional: true\n\n    # === Cargo Bloat ===\n    # Runs cargo-bloat to enforce the 4 MB binary size budget.\n    #\n    # This is a Tier 1 hard gate from Quality Targets. Binary size > 4 MB blocks\n    # PR merge. Without this gate, binary size regressions silently slip past code\n    # review and risk breaking the R2 target (single-page PDF extraction in < 100ms\n    # on a 1.6 GHz CPU, which requires a small binary to fit in CPU cache).\n    #\n    # Bead: pdftract-2rf\n    # Plan section: Phase 0.4 Quality Targets\n    #\n    # Enforcement policy:\n    # - Binary size (stripped) must be <= 4,194,304 bytes (4 MB) for x86_64-unknown-linux-musl\n    # - Other targets (macOS, Windows) are informational (not gated) due to larger metadata\n    # - Output is published as bloat-report.json artifact for historical tracking\n    # - A second invocation with --features remote tracks ureg contribution (PB-5 data)\n    #\n    # If budget exceeded, the first-line response is PB-2: switch wordlist to Bloom filter\n    # behind the wordlist-bloom feature (documented in ADR-002).\n    - name: cargo-bloat\n      activeDeadlineSeconds: 600\n      container:\n        image: ronaldraygun/pdftract-test-glibc:1.78\n        command: [bash, -c]\n        args:\n          - |\n            set -eo pipefail\n\n            echo \"==========================================\"\n            echo \"Cargo Bloat (Binary Size Budget)\"\n            echo \"==========================================\"\n\n            cd /workspace\n            export CARGO_HOME=\"/cache/cargo/registry\"\n            export CARGO_TARGET_DIR=\"/cache/cargo/target-bloat\"\n\n            # Install cargo-bloat if not present\n            if ! command -v cargo-bloat &> /dev/null; then\n              echo \"Installing cargo-bloat...\"\n              cargo install cargo-bloat --locked\n            fi\n\n            echo \"=== Running cargo bloat (default features, gated) ===\"\n            echo \"Target: x86_64-unknown-linux-musl\"\n            echo \"Budget: 4 MB (4,194,304 bytes)\"\n\n            # Build release binary first for accurate analysis\n            cargo build --release --target x86_64-unknown-linux-musl --features default --locked\n\n            # Run cargo bloat and capture output\n            cargo bloat --release --features default --crates --target x86_64-unknown-linux-musl -n 50 \\\n              > /tmp/bloat-default.txt 2>&1 || true\n\n            # Parse binary size from output\n            # cargo-bloat output format: \"File: pdftract X MB\"\n            BINARY_PATH=\"target/x86_64-unknown-linux-musl/release/pdftract\"\n            if [ ! -f \"$BINARY_PATH\" ]; then\n              echo \"ERROR: Binary not found at $BINARY_PATH\" >&2\n              exit 1\n            fi\n\n            # Get stripped binary size\n            STRIPPED_SIZE=$(x86_64-linux-musl-strip -o /tmp/pdftract-stripped \"$BINARY_PATH\" 2>/dev/null && stat -c%s /tmp/pdftract-stripped || stat -c%s \"$BINARY_PATH\")\n            BUDGET=4194304  # 4 MB\n\n            echo \"=== Binary size analysis ===\"\n            echo \"Stripped size: $STRIPPED_SIZE bytes\"\n            echo \"Budget: $BUDGET bytes\"\n            echo \"Remaining: $((BUDGET - STRIPPED_SIZE)) bytes\"\n\n            # Generate JSON report\n            cat > /workspace/bloat-report.json <<EOF\n            {\n              \"timestamp\": \"$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)\",\n              \"commit_sha\": \"{{workflow.parameters.commit-sha}}\",\n              \"target\": \"x86_64-unknown-linux-musl\",\n              \"features\": \"default\",\n              \"stripped_size_bytes\": $STRIPPED_SIZE,\n              \"budget_bytes\": $BUDGET,\n              \"within_budget\": $( [ \"$STRIPPED_SIZE\" -le \"$BUDGET\" ] && echo \"true\" || echo \"false\" ),\n              \"raw_output\": $(jq -R -s '.' < /tmp/bloat-default.txt)\n            }\n            EOF\n\n            # Check against budget\n            if [ \"$STRIPPED_SIZE\" -gt \"$BUDGET\" ]; then\n              echo \"==========================================\"\n              echo \"CARGO BLOAT CHECK FAILED\"\n              echo \"==========================================\"\n              echo \"Binary size exceeds 4 MB budget\"\n              echo \"Size: $STRIPPED_SIZE bytes\"\n              echo \"Budget: $BUDGET bytes\"\n              echo \"Over: $((STRIPPED_SIZE - BUDGET)) bytes\"\n              echo \"\"\n              echo \"First-line response (per PB-2):\"\n              echo \"  Switch wordlist to Bloom filter behind wordlist-bloom feature.\"\n              echo \"  See ADR-002 for implementation guidance.\"\n              echo \"\"\n              echo \"Top contributors:\"\n              head -30 /tmp/bloat-default.txt || true\n              echo \"\"\n              echo \"See bloat-report.json artifact for full details.\"\n              exit 1\n            fi\n\n            echo \"=== Running cargo bloat (remote features, informational) ===\"\n            echo \"This tracks ureg's contribution for PB-5 (alt-feature escape hatch)\"\n            cargo bloat --release --features remote --crates --target x86_64-unknown-linux-musl -n 50 \\\n              > /tmp/bloat-remote.txt 2>&1 || true\n\n            # Append remote feature data to report\n            if command -v jq &> /dev/null; then\n              jq --arg remote \"$(jq -R -s '.' < /tmp/bloat-remote.txt)\" \\\n                '. + {\"remote_features_raw\": $remote}' /workspace/bloat-report.json \\\n                > /tmp/bloat-report-merged.json && mv /tmp/bloat-report-merged.json /workspace/bloat-report.json\n            fi\n\n            echo \"=== Cargo bloat checks passed ===\"\n            echo \"Binary within 4 MB budget\"\n            echo \"Size: $STRIPPED_SIZE bytes ($(( STRIPPED_SIZE * 100 / BUDGET ))% of budget)\"\n\n            # Display top contributors for visibility\n            echo \"\"\n            echo \"Top 20 contributors:\"\n            head -30 /tmp/bloat-default.txt | tail -20 || true\n        volumeMounts:\n          - name: workspace\n            mountPath: /workspace\n          - name: cargo-cache\n            mountPath: /cache/cargo\n        resources:\n          requests:\n            cpu: 1000m\n            memory: 2Gi\n          limits:\n            cpu: 2000m\n            memory: 4Gi\n      outputs:\n        artifacts:\n          - name: bloat-report\n            path: /workspace/bloat-report.json\n\n    # === Memory Ceiling ===\n    # Runs memory ceiling tests to enforce RSS budgets.\n    #\n    # This is a Tier 1 hard gate from Quality Targets. Any document exceeding\n    # its memory budget blocks PR merge. Without this gate, memory regressions\n    # silently slip past code review and risk breaking the Memory targets.\n    #\n    # Bead: bf-1g1fd\n    # Plan section: Phase 0.4 Quality Targets - Memory targets\n    #\n    # Enforcement policy:\n    # - Peak RSS, 100-page vector PDF (buffered mode) < 512 MB\n    # - Peak RSS, streaming/NDJSON mode (any page count) < 256 MB\n    # - Peak RSS, adversarial fixtures < 1 GB hard ceiling\n    # - Output is published as memory-report.json artifact for historical tracking\n    # - Tests run under cgroup MemoryMax cap for clean failure mode\n    - name: memory-ceiling\n      activeDeadlineSeconds: 600\n      container:\n        image: ronaldraygun/pdftract-test-glibc:1.78\n        command: [bash, -c]\n        args:\n          - |\n            set -eo pipefail\n\n            echo \"==========================================\"\n            echo \"Memory Ceiling Tests\"\n            echo \"==========================================\"\n\n            cd /workspace\n            export CARGO_HOME=\"/cache/cargo/registry\"\n            export CARGO_TARGET_DIR=\"/cache/cargo/target-memory-ceiling\"\n\n            echo \"=== Running memory ceiling tests ===\"\n            echo \"Budgets:\"\n            echo \"  - Buffered 100-page: 512 MB\"\n            echo \"  - Streaming mode: 256 MB\"\n            echo \"  - Adversarial hard cap: 1024 MB\"\n            echo \"\"\n            echo \"Cgroup MemoryMax: 1536 MB (1.5 GB cap for clean failure)\"\n            echo \"  This enforces a hard ceiling on the entire test run.\"\n            echo \"  Individual document budgets are enforced by the harness.\"\n\n            #
